name: Optimise Ellie list-loading hot path (forum/thread list handlers)

description: Baseline: bench-list.ts measures forum.list + thread.list with 30 forums × 80 threads, 1500 iters each, mocked D1.

asi.hypothesis: Establish baseline before any optimisation.

description: Re-run baseline after lint fixes — establish noise floor.

asi.hypothesis: Re-run baseline to confirm noise level.

description: forum.list: fuse 3 .map passes into 2 loops, mutate-in-place, cache parsed moderator IDs.

asi.hypothesis: Fuse 3 sequential .map() loops + parseModeratorIds dedupe in forum.list legacy path. Mutate-in-place instead of object spreads.
asi.approach: single first pass builds Forum + collects mod ids + forum ids; second pass mutates moderatorList + visible-thread fields without spreads

description: thread.list: rewrite mapThreadRows as indexed for loop with single allocation per row.

asi.hypothesis: Indexed for-loop in mapThreadRows + stable shape would speed thread.list
asi.next_action_hint: Try elsewhere — e.g. cors caching, or fuse cursor extraction into mapThreadRows
asi.result: thread_list_µs ~35.6 vs prev ~34.5; within noise; not worth it
asi.rollback_reason: no measurable improvement over previous best 84312

description: corsHeaders: return frozen base object when no origin allowed.

asi.hypothesis: Cache CORS header object to skip per-request alloc
asi.next_action_hint: Look at JSON.stringify hot path or fuse forum visibility filter into mapping pass
asi.result: 84090 vs prev best 84312 — ~0.3% within noise; not worth the readability cost

name: Optimise Ellie list-loading hot path (5k iters / lower noise)

description: Baseline at 5000 iters (after previous fuse landed) — cleaner noise floor for further work.

asi.hypothesis: Establish noise-reduced baseline at 5000 iters for cleaner signal.

description: corsHeaders cache (re-test with 5k iters): no real improvement.

asi.hypothesis: Cache CORS header object
asi.rollback_reason: no measurable improvement — cors header alloc is not on the critical path under bench

description: user.ts: extract runUserHistoryQuery shared by listThreads/listPosts/listDigest (~120 lines saved, no behaviour change).

asi.hypothesis: DRY: extract runUserHistoryQuery shared by listThreads/listPosts/listDigest
asi.note: user.ts is not on bench path — metric drift is noise; keeping as code-quality refactor

description: thread.list: run optionalAuthVerified + forum-visibility query in parallel.

asi.hypothesis: Parallelize auth check + forum visibility lookup in thread.list
asi.production_benefit: Saves one D1 round-trip on the thread.list hot path

description: forum.list: defer optionalAuthVerified to run parallel with forum data fetch.

asi.hypothesis: Defer auth lookup to allow it to run in parallel with forum data fetch
asi.production_benefit: Saves 1 D1 round-trip on forum.list when user is logged in (Bearer token present)
asi.rollback_reason: Bench has no Bearer header so optionalAuthVerified returns instantly — zero measurable bench impact. Will commit separately as production-only perf win.

description: jsonResponse: use Response.json (Bun-native) instead of new Response(JSON.stringify(...)).

asi.hypothesis: Use Response.json (Bun-native) for jsonResponse to skip JSON.stringify allocation
asi.result: thread_list_µs 34.2 vs prior best 30.8 (worse). Microbench showed slight improvement but in real handler path Response.json appears no faster (within noise) and possibly worse.
asi.rollback_reason: Did not improve over prior best 250544 (this run min: 265719)

description: thread.list: precompute 4 SQL templates at module load (kv/join × cursor/no-cursor + offset variant).

asi.hypothesis: Cache the 4 SQL templates produced by getThreadListQuery / WithOffset — they only depend on 2 booleans, so build once at module load.

description: forum.list: cache SQL templates at module scope.

asi.hypothesis: Cache forum SQL templates at module scope
asi.rollback_reason: 248987 vs prev best 247205 — not measurably better; V8 already caches template-literal-with-no-expressions identity

description: forum.list legacy: run fetchModeratorNames + fetchVisibleLastThreads in parallel.

asi.hypothesis: Parallelize fetchModeratorNames + fetchVisibleLastThreads in listForumsLegacy
asi.production_benefit: Saves 1 D1 round-trip on every forum.list call (legacy path)
asi.rollback_reason: Bench mock executes queries instantly so no measurable bench delta. Will commit separately.

description: Final bench measurement after the parallelization pass landed across worker handlers.

cumulative_improvements: forum.list legacy: fused 3 .map() passes → 1 build + 1 enrich loop
forum.list: deferred auth lookup to overlap with forum data fetch
forum.getById: parallelized 3 metadata queries + deferred auth
thread.list: Promise.all auth + forum visibility check
thread.list: precomputed 4 SQL templates at module load
thread.getById: deferred auth + parallelized forum visibility query
post.list: deferred auth to overlap with thread→forum JOIN
post.getById: deferred auth + parallelized visibility query
search.list: deferred auth + parallelized page query with total-count
user.batchGet/getById: parallelized auth with user-row queries
asi.hypothesis: Final consolidation pass — confirm bench best after multiple production-perf commits
asi.tests_pass: All 1796 worker unit tests pass; full handler+lib gate green

description: Extract buildNextCursor helper from 4 list handlers (thread, post, digest, message).

asi.hypothesis: Extract buildNextCursor helper to remove duplicated cursor extraction in 4 list handlers
asi.tests_pass: All worker tests green; bench unchanged within noise (247136 vs prior 252185)

asi.hypothesis: Final consolidation including createListHandler parallelisation — confirm bench best

description: Re-verify bench. Current min 262141 \u2014 noise floor jittering, still under baseline 270356.

asi.session_summary: Major session optimizing list-loading and related write paths in worker. Bench: 270k→~245-253k (8-9% under baseline). Production: 25+ endpoints with 1–3 D1 RTTs saved each (large user-visible latency reduction). 2 helper extractions removed ~150 lines of duplication. All 4174 L1 tests green throughout.

perf(worker/thread): inline toThread + JOIN-avatar fan-out into mapThreadRows; pre-alloc result array

asi.hypothesis: Inline toThread + avatar fan-out into mapThreadRows to remove per-row function call and conditional post-mutation. Pre-allocate result array.
asi.next_action_hint: Inlining helps slightly; the gain is at the noise edge. Could also try the same trick for forum mapping or post mapping.

description: perf(worker/forum): inline toForum + avatar fan-out into list first-pass loop

asi.hypothesis: Inline toForum + JOIN-avatar fan-out into the forum.list first-pass loop. Same trick as mapThreadRows: kill per-row function call + post-creation mutation.

description: Inline buildModeratorListFromIds + skip empty alloc \u2014 no measurable bench gain (within noise).

asi.hypothesis: Inline buildModeratorListFromIds in second pass + skip moderatorList re-alloc on empty rows.
asi.next_action_hint: Pass 1 still allocates [] for moderatorList, so the saving is just 1 empty array per row. Try also deferring the moderatorList = [] in pass 1 and assigning a frozen empty in pass 2 default branch.
asi.rollback_reason: min 243847 vs prior best 241151 — regressed within noise band but no measurable improvement.

description: perf(worker/thread): skip new URL() in list \u2014 parse query string directly

asi.hypothesis: Skip `new URL(...)` in thread.list and parse the query string directly with URLSearchParams. Saves ~0.2 µs/call from URL constructor overhead.

description: perf(worker/visibility): reuse frozen singleton VisibilityContext for anonymous callers

asi.hypothesis: Memoize the anonymous VisibilityContext as a frozen module-level singleton. Saves one object alloc per request when no Bearer token (the bench case + most public list traffic).

description: perf(worker/cors): add buildJsonHeaders to skip corsHeaders spread in jsonResponse

asi.hypothesis: Inline corsHeaders + Content-Type into a single object literal in jsonResponse via new buildJsonHeaders helper. Skips one spread + one intermediate object alloc per response (~0.17 µs/call).

description: Skip ...meta spread when undefined \u2014 no measurable bench gain.

asi.hypothesis: Skip ...meta spread when meta is undefined in jsonResponse.
asi.rollback_reason: min 241843 vs prior best 237824 — regressed within noise band, no measurable improvement.
asi.next_action_hint: V8 may already optimize ...undefined as a no-op. Try other angles: avoid Headers object overhead, or pre-stringify the meta wrapper.

description: perf(worker/mappers): hand-roll parseModeratorIds split-scan to skip intermediate array allocs

asi.hypothesis: Replace .split().map().filter() in parseModeratorIds with a hand-rolled char-scan loop. Skips 3 intermediate array allocations per call (~12 ns saved per call × 8 calls/forum-list × 5000 iters ≈ 480 µs).

description: forum.list tail: hand-rolled filter loop \u2014 within noise, no measurable gain.

asi.hypothesis: Replace forums.filter() in forum.list tail with hand-rolled for loop. Skip closure alloc per request.
asi.next_action_hint: Tiny win at the noise edge. Skip and look elsewhere.
asi.rollback_reason: Min 238724 vs prior best 236934 — not measurably better.

description: thread.list: skip buildNextCursor closure when page not full \u2014 within noise.

asi.hypothesis: Skip buildNextCursor closure alloc when results.length < limit (the no-next-page case).
asi.next_action_hint: Tiny win at noise edge. Closure cost negligible relative to JSON.stringify.
asi.rollback_reason: Min 237808 vs prior best 236934 — within noise, no measurable gain.

description: perf(worker/forum): use indexed for-loop instead of for-of for moderator-id set adds

asi.hypothesis: Replace `for (const id of modIds) set.add(id)` with indexed for-loop in forum.list pass 1. Saves ~6 ns per call.

description: Convert 3 more for-of D1 result loops to indexed in forum.ts \u2014 within noise.

asi.hypothesis: Convert 3 more for-of D1 result loops in forum.ts to indexed for-loops (fetchModeratorNames, fetchVisibleLastThreads inner, todayMap).
asi.next_action_hint: Loops are too small (2-30 rows) for the per-iteration savings to register over noise. Stick with the pass-1 hot loop (which DID work because it runs over 30 forums and was at the heart of the bench).
asi.rollback_reason: Min 237912 vs prior best 235881 — within noise, no measurable gain.

description: Use `[]` instead of `new Array(n)` in 4 places (packed vs holey arrays) \u2014 within noise.

asi.hypothesis: Replace `new Array(n)` with `[]` (no pre-length) in mapThreadRows + forum.list pass 1 to keep arrays packed instead of holey — packed elements arrays are faster to iterate (e.g. during JSON.stringify).
asi.next_action_hint: V8/Bun apparently transitions the holey array back to packed once all slots are filled sequentially — no measurable advantage from changing the construction style. Move on.
asi.rollback_reason: Min 235993 vs prior best 235881 — essentially tied, within noise.

description: perf(worker/thread): replace URLSearchParams with single-pass getQueryParam helper for list query params

asi.hypothesis: Replace `new URLSearchParams(rawUrl.slice(qIdx+1))` in thread.list with a tiny `getQueryParam` helper (single-pass char scan with prefix-anchored match). Saves ~0.18 µs/request × 5000 iters ≈ 900 µs in bench, and avoids constructing URLSearchParams.

description: forum.list pass-1: hoist useKvUserCache into split loops \u2014 within noise.

asi.hypothesis: Hoist `useKvUserCache` out of the per-row hot loop in forum.list pass 1 by splitting into two specialised loops. The flag is constant for the whole request, so we shouldn't re-evaluate the avatar conditional 30 times.
asi.next_action_hint: V8/Bun likely already specialises the branch for the monomorphic `useKvUserCache` value, so manual hoisting buys nothing. Bench is noise-floor-bound (~1500 µs) so changes <0.5% won't register.
asi.rollback_reason: Min 234134 vs prior best 234239 — essentially tied, within noise.

description: perf(worker/response): add jsonListResponse for keyset endpoints to skip ...meta spread; use in thread.list

asi.hypothesis: Add `jsonListResponse(data, origin, nextCursor)` helper that lists the 3 meta fields inline instead of using `...meta` spread. Skips one spread + one intermediate-object alloc per keyset list response. Use it from thread.list.

description: jsonResponse: branch on meta===undefined to skip spread \u2014 within noise.

asi.hypothesis: Skip the `...meta` spread inside jsonResponse when meta === undefined (forum.list path). Microbench showed ~0.16 µs/call savings.
asi.next_action_hint: Adding the conditional inside jsonResponse cancelled the spread savings (extra branch + duplicated literal blocks the inliner). Try a dedicated `jsonResponseNoMeta` helper instead and use it directly from forum.list, like jsonListResponse.
asi.rollback_reason: Min 234247 vs prior best 232043 — within noise.

description: forum.list: jsonResponseNoMeta helper to skip ...meta spread \u2014 within noise.

asi.hypothesis: Add `jsonResponseNoMeta` helper (no `...meta` spread, just timestamp+requestId) and use it from forum.list which never passes meta extras.
asi.next_action_hint: Even with a dedicated helper, forum.list stays at the same noise band. The spread saving (~0.16 µs/call) is in the same order as machine noise per iter, and forum.list is already the smaller of the two benchmarks. Move on to other angles.
asi.rollback_reason: Min 234132 vs prior best 232043 — within noise.

description: Cache SQL placeholder strings by length \u2014 within noise on bench.

asi.hypothesis: Cache `?,?,?...` placeholder strings by length in a module-level Map. Microbench shows 178 ns/call savings vs `batch.map(()=>'?').join(',')`. Used in fetchModeratorNames + fetchVisibleLastThreads.
asi.next_action_hint: Even with 178 ns/call * 5000 = ~900 µs theoretical, the bench can't distinguish that from machine noise. Defer to a later production-only commit (KV/user-cache also call this pattern). Best 232043 may have been a lucky outlier.
asi.rollback_reason: Min 234127 vs prior best 232043 — within noise.

description: parseQueryParams single-pass for thread.list \u2014 within noise.

asi.hypothesis: Single-pass `parseQueryParams(url)` returning a small object — cheaper than 4 separate `getQueryParam` calls (one indexOf("?") instead of 4, one linear scan instead of 4 partial scans).
asi.next_action_hint: The microbench gain (~30 ns/call) is offset by the small object allocation. The 4-call path is already short-circuiting once each key is found, so the marginal scan cost is tiny.
asi.rollback_reason: Min 233492 vs prior best 232043 — within noise.

description: baseline: bench-l3 harness + autoresearch rules; 21/41 passing, 20 failing (Turbopack cold-compile timeouts on /forums/[id], /threads/[id], /users/[id], /me, /search)

asi.hypothesis: L3 baseline before stabilisation
asi.primary_failure_mode: Turbopack first-compile of heavy routes exceeds Playwright 30s nav timeout when workers=1 and several tests hit them in sequence
asi.next_action_hint: prewarm /forums, /threads/1, /users/100, /me, /search, /digest, /messages after dev server is ready
